steps.title: Interpret the percentage
steps.content:
When a material can contain a maximum of 0.002% phosphorus by mass, the mass of phosphorus is 0.002% of the total sample mass:
$$ \text{mass of P} = 0.002\% \times 160 \text{ g} $$

steps.title: Convert percentage to decimal
steps.content:
To compute with the percentage, convert it to decimal form by dividing by 100:
$$ 0.002\% = \frac{0.002}{100} = 0.00002 $$

steps.title: Calculate the maximum phosphorus mass
steps.content:
Now multiply the decimal form by the sample mass:
$$ \text{mass of P} = 0.00002 \times 160 = 0.0032 \text{ grams} $$
The maximum mass of phosphorus the sample can contain is 0.0032 grams.
